rules._comment: Escape hatch: disk-used % is (total-free)/total — not expressible in the structured query IR. The clickhouse.disks.free_space / clickhouse.disks.total_space metrics are emitted by a custom_queries block in the Datadog ClickHouse check (1-platform/2-monitoring/.../datadog/common/main.tf), tagged with disk_name and kube_cluster_name.

annotations.description: Warn when ClickHouse disk usage exceeds 80%; critical at 90%. Recovery at 75% (warn) / 85% (critical).

datadog.message: ClickHouse disk {{disk_name.name}} on {{host.name}} (cluster {{kube_cluster_name.name}}) has exceeded disk usage thresholds (warn 80%, critical 90%).

rules._comment: P0: complete ingestion halt — no rows inserted for 5 min. clickhouse.table.insert.row.total is a monotonic counter emitted by the standard Datadog ClickHouse integration; .as_count() gives the per-window delta so a zero delta means no rows inserted. Verified against live agent check output on the sandbox.

annotations.description: No rows have been inserted into ClickHouse for 5 minutes — possible ingestion pipeline halt.

datadog.message: ClickHouse on cluster {{kube_cluster_name.name}} has had zero row insertions for the past 5 minutes. Check the ingestion pipeline (Kafka consumer, OTel collector, or upstream producers).

rules._comment: P1: ingestion delay exceeds SLA — delayed inserts building up for 10+ min. clickhouse.query.insert.delayed counts INSERT queries currently queued/delayed due to back-pressure (e.g. max_insert_delay_ms). A sustained non-zero value signals pipeline back-pressure exceeding SLA. For true end-to-end delay (now()-max(event_time)), add a custom_query to main.tf against a known ingestion table. Verified metric name against live agent check.

annotations.description: ClickHouse has delayed INSERT queries — back-pressure may be causing ingestion delay exceeding SLA.

datadog.message: ClickHouse on cluster {{kube_cluster_name.name}} has delayed INSERT queries for 10+ minutes. Investigate ingestion pipeline back-pressure.

rules._comment: P2: minor ingestion slowdown — insert row rate slightly below baseline. clickhouse.table.insert.row.total delta over 15m below a threshold indicates a slowdown not yet causing delays. The threshold (rows per 15m window) must be calibrated per environment; placeholder is 50. Verified metric name against live agent check.

annotations.description: ClickHouse insert row rate is running below the expected baseline — minor slowdown, not yet at SLA breach. Threshold needs env-specific calibration.

datadog.message: ClickHouse on cluster {{kube_cluster_name.name}} insert rate is below the minor slowdown threshold. No immediate action required but monitor for escalation.

rules._comment: P0: failed INSERT queries. Uses clickhouse.inserts.failed, emitted by a custom_query gated on monitoring.metrics.writeFailureQuery.enabled in divyam-helm-charts/charts/clickhouse (COUNT of system.query_log rows with query_kind='Insert' and type IN ExceptionBeforeStart/ExceptionWhileProcessing over the trailing 5m); 0 when healthy. Replaces the previous clickhouse.log.messages.error.total query, which thresholded a CUMULATIVE GAUGE of all error-level log messages — its value is >0 as soon as any error is ever logged (e.g. benign startup Keeper/network errors), so the monitor fired permanently and never measured new/insert-specific failures. Threshold 0 = any insert failure in the window; max() (not .as_count()) since the SQL already windows the count.

annotations.description: ClickHouse INSERT queries are failing (system.query_log insert exceptions in the last 5m). Investigate immediately — ingestion/billing data may be lost.

datadog.message: ClickHouse on cluster {{kube_cluster_name.name}} has failed INSERT queries in the last 5 minutes. Check system.query_log WHERE query_kind='Insert' AND type LIKE 'Exception%' for the failing inserts.
